commit.message:
security: add CODEX_API_KEY to SecretRedactor known values

The new CODEX_API_KEY field was not collected by SecretRedactor._collect_secrets().
If an operator sets a codex-specific key different from AI_API_KEY, it would not
be redacted from outgoing messages (only pattern-based detection would catch it).

Added settings.ai.codex.codex_api_key to the candidates list and a regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Your AI CLI, anywhere. Chat with GitHub Copilot, Codex, OpenAI or Anthropic via Telegram or Slack — one container per project.

commit.message:
Replace /delete with /exit: close current session in one step

/exit deletes the current session and switches to the most recently
active one (or creates a new session if it was the last). This removes
the friction of needing to switch away before deleting, which
discouraged starting fresh sessions.

/delete is kept as a legacy alias that points to /exit and /prune.
The session picker submenu for /delete is removed; /exit takes no
arguments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add configurable hostname and admin user password (#187)

* feat(bootstrap): add configurable hostname and admin user password

- Add `hostname` and `admin_user_password` to config.toml template
- Fix /etc/hosts to properly map 127.0.0.1→localhost and 127.0.1.1→hostname
- Set admin user password via password_hash('sha512') for Cockpit login
- Require `hostname` in bootstrap playbook config validation

* docs: update documentation for hostname and admin_user_password config

Update bootstrap, config, and getting-started docs to reflect new
config.toml settings. Clarify admin_user_password is optional (for
Cockpit login) while hostname is required.

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails (#189)

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails instead of missing auth.log

* docs(fail2ban): document jail configuration and systemd backend usage

* docs update

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ansible_user): decouple admin user creation from password setting

Admin user creation was gated on admin_user_password being set, which
skipped user creation entirely when password was empty. Split into two
tasks: always create user, conditionally set password. Also use
update_password: on_create for idempotent reruns.

* fix(bootstrap): tighten /etc/hosts regex to avoid prefix matching

^127\.0\.0\.1 also matches 127.0.0.10. Add \s after the IP to ensure
only the exact address followed by whitespace is matched.

* test(ansible_runner): assert hostname in bootstrap required keys

* docs: clarify admin_user_password is optional across all docs

Mark admin_user_password as optional (only needed for Cockpit login)
in first-deployment, bootstrap, secrets, and cockpit documentation.

* docs: clarify optional password and fallback in layers and cockpit docs

* fix(bootstrap): add hostname validation before VPS connection

* fix(ansible_user): add no_log to password task to prevent secret leakage

* chore: sync config.example.toml with TEMPLATE in user_config.rs

Adds missing keys: hostname, admin_user_password, bichon_*,
headscale_subdomain, paperless_subdomain, paperless_tailscale_ip,
restic_*, tailscale_login_server, and vdirsyncer_*.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ghostty): pre-warm D-Bus portal to eliminate 10s first-launch delay

Ghostty is built with -Dflatpak=true, which enables an internal D-Bus
bridge to org.freedesktop.Flatpak for host-process spawning. On first
launch after boot, if flatpak-session-helper is not yet running, D-Bus
must cold-start it synchronously — taking ~10 seconds.

Add ghostty-wrapper.sh as the Flatpak command. It fires a non-blocking
gdbus StartServiceByName call for org.freedesktop.Flatpak in the
background before exec-ing ghostty, giving the portal service a head
start so it is ready by the time ghostty needs it.

If the delay persists after this fix, it is likely GTK4 shader
compilation; test with GSK_RENDERER=cairo and add --env=GSK_RENDERER=gl
to finish-args if confirmed. Diagnosis commands documented in GOTCHAS.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix notification-click race: stale initSession overriding session switch

When tapping a push notification while SSE reconnect is in flight,
initSession() could read the old URL hash, load the old session, and
set state.sessionId back to  silently rejecting the navigateit
handler's session_created event via the session guard. This caused
messages to go to the wrong session, and refreshing did not help
because the hash was also overwritten.

Add a sessionSwitchGen counter: user-initiated switches (notification
click, /switch) increment it; initSession/resumeAndLoad capture the
value before async work and bail out if it changed mid-flight.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: harden CI workflows against injection and privilege escalation

review-gate.yml:
- CRITICAL: replace pull_request_target with pull_request (was exposing
  secrets to fork PRs)
- Add explicit permissions: pull-requests + issues write only

finalize.yml:
- CRITICAL: tighten PR body regex from [^\s]+ to [a-zA-Z0-9_\-]+\.md
  (prevents path traversal / injection via crafted PR body)
- Remove GITHUB_TOKEN from finalizer step env (only needed for release)
- Use core.setOutput() instead of return (correct github-script API)
- Add explicit permissions: contents write only

docs-review.yml + implement-ci.yml:
- Add explicit least-privilege permissions blocks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement 13 critical gaps: backtester, odds engine, correlation modeling, rotation tracker, validators, logger, game log cache, rate limiter, st.cache_data, and system health dashboard

- engine/backtester.py: historical backtest engine (Monte Carlo vs game logs)
- engine/odds_engine.py: American odds ↔ probability, true edge, EV, vig
- engine/rotation_tracker.py: minutes trend/role-change tracking, trend factor

- data/data_manager.py: st.cache_data decorators, clear_all_caches(), get_data_health_report()
- data/platform_fetcher.py: odds helper functions after _now_str()
- data/game_log_cache.py: persistent JSON cache for player game logs
- utils/logger.py: rotating-file + console structured logger
- utils/rate_limiter.py: token-bucket rate limiter with circuit breaker

feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles (#181)

* Initial plan

* feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dns_record): add input validation, error handling, and zone ID caching

- Assert required variables upfront for clear failure messages
- Add failed_when checking Cloudflare API success field to catch
  logical failures returned with HTTP 200
- Add changed_when for accurate idempotency reporting
- Use verbosity-gated no_log so errors are visible with -vvv
- Cache zone ID across multiple role inclusions in same play
- Add meta/main.yml matching project conventions

* docs: update DNS documentation for automatic provisioning

DNS A records are now auto-provisioned during app deployment via the
dns_record role. Update first-deployment guide, managing-records,
batch-operations, cloudflare-setup, infrastructure-layers, ansible-roles
guide, and troubleshooting docs to reflect this change.

* feat(cockpit): add dns_record integration with Tailscale IP

Cockpit binds to the Tailscale interface, so its DNS A record must
point to the Tailscale IP rather than the public VPS IP.

* fix(dns_record): validate Cloudflare success flag on all API calls

- Add failed_when on zone lookup and record lookup GET requests
- Check for exactly 1 zone result instead of just non-empty
- Use verbosity-gated no_log on assert task

* fix(dns_record): domain-scoped cache, IPv4 validation, drift detection

- Cache zone IDs in a dict keyed by domain (safe for multi-domain plays)
- Validate dns_record_ip matches IPv4 format in assert block
- Fail explicitly when multiple A records found for same FQDN
- Update task now triggers on IP, TTL, or proxied changes (not just IP)

* docs: add cockpit to integrated apps lists, fix troubleshooting causes

- Include cockpit in all dns_record integration app lists
- Broaden batch-operations example to include all non-integrated apps
- Rewrite troubleshooting causes to reflect realistic failure scenarios

* fix(dns_record): strict IPv4 octet-range validation, update docs example

- Tighten regex to reject out-of-range octets (e.g. 999.999.999.999)
- Update managing-records example to show only non-integrated apps

* fix(dns_record): prevent token leakage, use idiomatic regex test

- Keep no_log: true on all URI tasks to prevent Bearer token exposure
  in CI logs or shared terminals at any verbosity level
- Surface Cloudflare API errors via separate fail tasks that log only
  sanitized json.errors field
- Switch is regex() to is match() for Ansible idiom consistency

* fix(dns_record): include HTTP status and msg fallback in error messages

All fail tasks now surface status code and Ansible's msg field when
Cloudflare json.errors is unavailable (timeouts, TLS errors, non-JSON
responses).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <me@sripwoud.xyz>

commit.message:
fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language (#667)

* fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language

Two consecutive code blocks with the same language (e.g., both `mjs`)
were incorrectly grouped into a CJS/MJS switchable tab. Add a check
to ensure the two code blocks have different languages before creating
a switchable code tab.

Refs: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62181

commit.message:
Add terminal chart rendering for Kusto render queries (#22)

* Surface Kusto render metadata

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Render Kusto charts in the terminal

Add opt-in `query --chart` support that renders compatible Kusto render metadata as Hex1b terminal charts in human output and Mermaid charts in markdown output. The CLI now surfaces compatibility hints and unsupported messages, rejects `--format json --chart`, and includes tests and docs for the new behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix chart rendering review issues

Address the substantive issues found in review by rejecting sparse grouped charts instead of fabricating zeroes, honoring stacked line layouts for human rendering, treating case-distinct grouped values separately, waiting for stable Hex1b output, and sanitizing Mermaid control characters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(house-forecast): finalize increment 6 docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: deploy `Cockpit` for web-based monitoring and control (#180)

* Initial plan

* feat: deploy Cockpit for web-based monitoring and control

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cockpit): maintain alphabetical role ordering in apps playbook

* docs(cockpit): trim verbose sections and fix misleading config.toml reference

Remove inaccurate claim that cockpit variables are overridable via
config.toml — they are standard Ansible role defaults. Remove redundant
Features and Security sections (covered by the intro and Access section).

* docs(cockpit): add to applications overview table

* feat(cockpit): proxy through Caddy with real TLS instead of direct port access

Replace direct port 9090 access (self-signed cert + UFW rule) with the
same Caddy reverse proxy pattern used by paperless:
- Bind cockpit.socket to localhost only via systemd drop-in override
- AllowUnencrypted in cockpit.conf so Caddy handles all TLS
- Caddy Caddyfile bound to Tailscale IP with DNS-01 cert
- Remove UFW rule (no longer needed with localhost binding)

commit.message:
Custom dashboards and unified ai no playground (#1243)

This PR implements unified AI endpoint and custom dashboards. 

**Unified AI Endpoint**
We now use a single endpoint throughout the codebase that makes the call
to openrouter. Specifically, email drafts, email templates, email
themes, wysiwyg, cmd centre ai search and docs ai, all use this unified
ai endpoint. All the tools are defined in the backend, all the prompts
exist in the backend.

How to review this PR for unified ai endpoint:

This PR will be easier to review if we look at the different folders
that were affected.

under packages - We added streaming functionality, and made renaming
changes

under docs - there are three files that have changed
package.json - we updated the package (we were previously using a very
old version of the package)
route.ts - we changed the call from a direct call to openrouter to the
unified ai endpoint
ai-chat.tsx - because of updating the package, we had to make changes to
adapt to the latest versions of the package

under backend 
route.ts - the main unified ai endpoint. this endpoint uses various
support files
forward.ts - this is the forward to production functionality
models.ts - consists of the models, and the rules for selecting those
models
prompts.ts - consists of the base prompt + specific system prompts
depending upon the usage
schema.ts
every single file under ai/tools folder - which as the name suggests,
consists of the implementations of the different tools that can be
provided to the llm
route-handlers - added support for streaming to SmartRoute and response
under dashboard

ai-search/route.ts - refactored the file to use unified ai endpoint
chat-adapters.ts - refactored the file to use unified ai endpoint and
created extra checks for the ai generated code

**Custom Dashboards**
We let the user write their query in english. We then use AI to create
dashboards that are interactive, live and savable. This PR includes a
new package called dashboard-ui-components. This package has components
that are used in the dashboard and more importantly, these components
are being imported from esm in the ai generated code for custom
dashboards. We also change the bar at the top for the products pages.

How to review this PR:

Review the new package (package/dashboard-ui-components), the setup and
the files inside it.
Review the schema changes in stack-shared/src
Review the changes in dashboard. The following changes have been made
Updated the design-components folder since we moved the dashboard
components to the new package
Updated imports for these components accordingly
Updated the title bar of the product pages
Created the files for custom dashboards under the dashboards folder and
components under commands/create-dashboard
Created a script under dashboard/scripts that generates the file with
type definitions that would go to the llm
Review the backend
Started using unified ai endpoint

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added custom dashboards feature allowing users to create and manage
personalized dashboards with AI assistance.
* Integrated AI-assisted dashboard code generation with visual preview
and editing capabilities.
* Introduced new AI query endpoints supporting stream and generate modes
with configurable model quality/speed settings.

* **Improvements**
* Reorganized UI components into a dedicated component library package
for better code reuse.
* Enhanced chat architecture with improved message handling and tool
integration.
* Updated AI provider integration with improved configuration
management.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

commit.message:
fix: use Python for SSH key parsing to handle literal \n in secret

The SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret stores newlines as literal \n characters.
Bash sed/echo have escaping issues in YAML. Python handles this reliably.
Also added key validation with clear error message on failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: d-asa
repository.description: Debt Algorand Standard Application

commit.message:
poller: fix parser O(N) deletions, decouple MQTT publish, use selectors

- GVRETParser: read-pointer + periodic compaction instead of del per frame
- MQTT: daemon thread draining a SimpleQueue; main recv loop never blocks on publish
- Socket: selectors.select() replaces timeout polling, drains all buffered data per wakeup
- seen dict stores (count, data, decoded) so decode_201 is called once per frame
- redraw: single write() call instead of multiple prints

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle SSH key stored as single line with literal \n

The SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret has newlines stored as literal \n characters,
resulting in a 1-line key file. Added sed to convert them back.
